title: End-to-end training and evaluation pipeline for Parameter Golf lane_3. Show (1)
caption: End-to-end training and evaluation pipeline for Parameter Golf lane_3. Show (1) float-only training leading to Baseline I (float model + post-hoc int8 quantization + zlib); (2) staged QAT path with fake quantization (qat_start_frac, qat_group_size) leading to QAT models E/F/H; (3) the common evaluation path applying int8 quantization + zlib roundtrip to produce val_bpb and runtime metrics. Indicate the 16MB artifact limit and the quick-gate runtime<=1.10x constraint on the evaluation branch.
label: fig:pipeline_overview_1

title: Conceptual diagram of the model and gate structure used in Parameter Golf. Depic
caption: Conceptual diagram of the model and gate structure used in Parameter Golf. Depict a generic stack of layers (e.g., transformer blocks or conv blocks) with per-layer channels/heads and an optional gating mask. Highlight where quantization operators (fake quant, int8) are inserted during QAT, and which tensors are subject to zlib-based measurements (weights vs activations). Annotate the gate budget (fraction of channels/heads active) and clarify that inference uses deterministic gates while training can use stochastic gates.
label: fig:architecture_diagram_2

title: Detailed schematic of the codec-guided distillation objective (Hypothesis 1). Sh
caption: Detailed schematic of the codec-guided distillation objective (Hypothesis 1). Show: float teacher path producing logits and reference tensors; student path with fake-quantized weights/activations; computation of L_task and KL distillation loss from teacher logits; selection of specific quantized tensors (e.g., final-layer weights or activations), conversion to bytes, zlib.compress call, computation of compressed bits-per-byte; comparison against a teacher- or baseline-derived target alpha to form L_comp=(L_zlib-alpha)^2; and final combination L_total=L_task+lambda_kd*L_KD+lambda_comp*L_comp. Include a small inset showing sparse evaluation of L_comp every N steps on CPU.
label: fig:method_flowchart_3

title: Visualization of compression-biased stochastic gating (Hypothesis 2). For a sing
caption: Visualization of compression-biased stochastic gating (Hypothesis 2). For a single representative layer, depict multiple channels/heads with associated EMA statistics h_c (zlib bpb per channel). Show periodic measurement: activations are quantized, split per channel, passed through zlib, yielding bpb_c and updating h_c. Then illustrate how p_drop(c) is computed from h_c (higher bpb -> higher drop probability) and used to sample binary gates g_c during training. Emphasize that at inference all gates are fixed to 1 (no stochasticity) and the graph matches the baseline.
label: fig:concept_illustration_4

title: Validation bpb for Float-only vs. QAT+Roundtrip-aligned Models
caption: Comparison of validation bits-per-byte (val_bpb) between the baseline float-only training and the proposed staged QAT aligned to int8+zlib roundtrip under the same gate budget and artifact size limit. Lower val_bpb indicates better compression, illustrating whether the QAT+roundtrip-aware alignment improves post-roundtrip performance relative to the float baseline.
label: fig:fig_main_results_comparison

title: Runtime Overhead and Quick-gate Constraint
caption: Runtime comparison between the baseline float-only model and the QAT+roundtrip-aligned model, showing absolute runtime (ms) and the runtime ratio relative to baseline. A dashed horizontal reference at 1.10× marks the quick-gate threshold, indicating whether the proposed method satisfies the runtime constraint while improving val_bpb.
label: fig:fig_runtime_overhead_quick_gate

title: Bitrate–Runtime Trade-off for Baseline vs. QAT+Roundtrip
caption: Joint view of compression efficiency and computational cost, plotting val_bpb against runtime for the baseline float-only and QAT+roundtrip-aligned models. Points lying toward the lower-left corner correspond to configurations that are both more efficient (lower val_bpb) and faster (lower runtime), highlighting the trade-off frontier under the artifact size and quick-gate constraints.
label: fig:fig_bitrate_vs_runtime_tradeoff
